perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which is designated as 'confidential' o r – where communicated orally – is designated as confidential or referred to similarly when communic ated. Oral communications must be confirmed subsequently in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this non-disclosure agreement, the term 'confidential information' means information which may or may not be designated as 'confidential' and oral communications do not require further confirmation in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty into the definition of 'confidential information'. Originally, it was clearly defined as information designated as confidential, especially when oral. The modification introduces ambiguity by suggesting that designation is optional and removes the requirement for written confirmation of oral communications. This contradicts the original, stricter definition of what constitutes 'confidential information' later on in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term 'confidential information' includes, but i s not limited to, all documents, drawings, data, subjects, information, records, knowhow, protected rights, inventions, ideas, concepts, knowledge, technology, design, specifications, including dimen sions, tolerances, materials and connections, blueprints, diagrams, software, improvements, produ ction procedures, production processes, formulae, all documents and information concerning the company, in particular administrative and commercial facts and details concerning customers, products, suppliers and other business partners and any and all documents and information insofar a s it relates to the collaboration proposed which is disclosed during the term of the contract and is accessible only to a limited circle of persons or where the DISCLOSER intends that it should only be available to a limited circle of persons, however that information may be disclosed, e.g. in writing, orally, by telephone, encoded, presented in graphi c form, etc. and whatever the media and recording pro cess used, e.g. optically, electronically or magnetically. The DISCLOSEE for the purposes of this agreement i s the DISCLOSEE as defined above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'confidential information' by making its designation optional and removing the requirement for written confirmation of oral communications. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which broadly defines 'confidential information' and includes oral communications without suggesting optional designation or removal of written confirmation. The contradicted text assumes a stricter, more inclusive definition of 'confidential information' and the changed text undermines this assumption.

perturbation.original_text: The DISCLOSEE agrees to keep any and all written, e lectronic and oral information and models conveyed or made available to it directly or indire ctly in the course of the collaboration strictly in confidence and not to disclose it or make it availa ble to any third party without the DISCLOSER's consent in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE agrees to endeavor to keep written, electronic, and oral information confidential, but shall only be responsible for disclosing information deemed confidential, but not oral or electronic. The DISCLOSEE is allowed to share information to related third parties without any approval in writing from the DISCLOSER.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates strict confidentiality for all information. This modification suggests that the DISCLOSEE only needs to make an effort to keep written information confidential and the DISCLOSEE is allowed to share information to related third parties, directly contradicting the original requirement for strict confidentiality and consent for disclosure to any third party. This could cause disputes over what information the DISCLOSEE is actually obligated to protect and the DISCLOSEE to share infomation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The DISCLOSEE agrees to keep any and all written, e lectronic and oral information and models conveyed or made available to it directly or indire ctly in the course of the collaboration strictly in confidence and not to disclose it or make it availa ble to any third party without the DISCLOSER's consent in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates strict confidentiality and requires written consent for any disclosure to third parties. The changed text allows the DISCLOSEE to share information with related third parties without written consent, directly contradicting the strict confidentiality and consent requirements outlined in the contradicted text. The changed text also limits confidentiality to only written information deeming oral and electronic information not confidential. The contradicted text and original text both state that all information: 'written, electronic, and oral' must be kept confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The DISCLOSEE may not make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation other than to staff who necessarily re quire it to perform their duties. Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE is allowed to make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation regardless if it needs to perform their duties. The DISCLOSEE will not be held liable for any losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause restricts access to confidential information within the DISCLOSEE's organization to only those staff who need it, and emphasizes that these individuals must have confidentiality agreements in place. The modified version contradicts this by stating that the DISCLOSEE is allowed to make confidential information available to anyone within its enterprise, regardless of their need for the information, and the DISCLOSEE will not be held liable for losses the DISCLOSER suffers. This creates a direct conflict regarding the scope of information access and responsibility for breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The DISCLOSEE may not make confidential information available within its enterprise or organisation other than to staff who necessarily re quire it to perform their duties. Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text restricts access to confidential information to only those within the organization who need it and are bound by confidentiality agreements. The changed text allows access to anyone within the organization, regardless of need, and removes liability for losses, which directly opposes the contradicted text's restriction and liability clause. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential inform ation provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE cannot deduce any rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of ap plying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.changed_text: Any confidential information created as a result of this agreement will become jointly owned and the DISCLOSEE may deduce rights from knowing the information.
perturbation.explanation: The original states that all confidential information is the property of Fronius, and that the DISCLOSEE cannot derive any rights from it. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that confidential information created as a result of the agreement will be jointly owned, and that the DISCLOSEE may deduce rights from knowing the information, creating uncertainty about the ownership and usage rights of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential inform ation provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE cannot deduce any rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of ap plying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that all confidential information is the property of Fronius and the DISCLOSEE cannot derive any rights from it. The changed text states that confidential information created as a result of the agreement will be jointly owned and the DISCLOSEE may deduce rights from knowing the information. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all documents containing confidential infor mation, copies of such documents and extracts the DISCLOSEE makes from such documents and models the DISCLOSER provides must be returned or demonstrably destroyed and their destru ction be confirmed in writing should the DISCLOSER so require.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE is not required to return documents containing confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the clause that requires the DISCLOSEE to return or destroy confidential information documents. The new clause states the DISCLOSEE is not required to return the documents, creating uncertainty about the DISCLOSEE's obligations regarding the handling of these materials. This contradicts with the original requirement of returning all documents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any and all documents containing confidential infor mation, copies of such documents and extracts the DISCLOSEE makes from such documents and models the DISCLOSER provides must be returned or demonstrably destroyed and their destru ction be confirmed in writing should the DISCLOSER so require.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states the DISCLOSEE is not required to return confidential information documents, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that mandates the DISCLOSEE to return or destroy such documents if the DISCLOSER requires it. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the DISCLOSEE's obligations, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the definition of structural flaws causing in-text contradiction when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement enters into force when signed by all contracting parties, and applies for a term of … years. The agreement is engrossed in duplicate. Any amendments or additions to this agreement mus t be in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement may be terminated by either party with no prior notice and applies indefinitely. Any amendments or additions to this agreement can be made verbally and is not required in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes a fixed term and requires written amendments. The modified version contradicts this by stating the agreement applies indefinitely and any amendments can be made verbally. This directly conflicts with the original clause that defines the agreement's duration and method of amendment, leading to disputes over the agreement's term and enforceability of modifications. This will create uncertainty in the execution and enforcement of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This agreement enters into force when signed by all contracting parties, and applies for a term of … years. The agreement is engrossed in duplicate. Any amendments or additions to this agreement mus t be in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement can be terminated with no prior notice and applies indefinitely, which directly contradicts the original text stating the agreement has a fixed term. Furthermore, the changed text allows for verbal amendments, while the original text requires amendments to be in writing. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the agreement's duration and method of amendment are fundamentally altered and conflict with the contradicted text.
